app.// withGlobalTauri: Disabled: exposing the global Tauri API on window is a security foot-gun (any script on the page can call native commands). The app uses the scoped @tauri-apps/api imports instead.

security.// csp: Tight production CSP. 'unsafe-eval' is intentionally absent; Vite-HMR-mode uses tauri dev which bypasses this bundle. Inline styles stay allowed because Radix/Tailwind inject them at runtime. connect-src is https:/wss: only to block mixed-content downgrades. frame-src allows blob: for WebContainer preview iframes.
security.csp: default-src 'self' tauri:; script-src 'self' tauri:; script-src-attr 'none'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https:; img-src 'self' data: blob: https:; font-src 'self' data: https:; connect-src 'self' https: wss: ipc: http://ipc.localhost tauri:; worker-src 'self' blob:; frame-src 'self' blob: https:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'

bundle.longDescription: bolt.gives — AI coding agent desktop app with WebContainer + hosted runtime support.

macOS.// minimumSystemVersion: Intentionally set — some Tauri 2 plugins require 10.15+.

plugins.// updater: pubkey MUST be filled before a production release. Empty pubkey triggers fail-closed behavior in main.rs (see docs/DESKTOP_UPDATER.md). Generate via: pnpm dlx @tauri-apps/cli signer generate -w ~/.tauri/bolt.gives.key
